stories.title: Wavelength-multiplexed diffractive optical storage enables massively parallel image retrieval
stories.desc: The explosive growth of data generated by artificial intelligence, cloud computing and modern digital infrastructure is placing increasing pressure on existing information storage technologies. Although magnetic storage systems such as hard disk drives remain the dominant platform for digital…

stories.title: Long-theorized electron-on-helium qubit achieves strong coupling to a single microwave photon
stories.desc: Quantum computers, devices that store and process information leveraging the principles of quantum mechanics, have been found to be promising for tackling some problems that cannot be solved by classical computers. Quantum computers store data in the form of qubits (i.e., quantum bits), units of…

stories.title: Quantum vacuum could help break molecular bonds with less energy, simulations suggest
stories.desc: A team of researchers led by Felipe Herrera, a professor at the University of Santiago and a researcher at the Millennium Institute for Research in Optics (MIRO), has identified a quantum phenomenon that enables chemical bonds to be broken using significantly less energy than is normally required.

stories.title: Space sensor could spot hidden nuclear weapons in orbit with 99% accuracy
stories.desc: In 2024, a U.S. government official warned that Russia could be developing a new satellite designed to carry nuclear weapons into space. The statement followed the launch of a suspicious Russian satellite into low-Earth orbit in 2022, just a few weeks before the country's full-scale invasion of…

stories.title: Why some glasses break suddenly while others deform smoothly
stories.desc: If a liquid is cooled slowly to its freezing point, it becomes a crystal in which the constituent particles are arranged in an ordered pattern. In contrast, when the liquid is cooled very quickly, the particles are unable to arrange themselves in an ordered fashion, and it becomes glass. Glassy…

stories.title: X-pinch plasma achieves radial proton acceleration for crisp imaging
stories.desc: Plasma pinches: From pursuits of nuclear fusion to an attractive point source of accelerated protons for proton radiography.

stories.title: Is Life Just Different?
stories.desc: The idea of ‘biological agency’ — that life devises its own goals and behaves accordingly — complicates our understanding of what it means to be alive. But does it serve a scientific purpose? The post Is Life Just Different? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

stories.title: Cottonwood Fire Chars Utah
stories.desc: The blaze burned more than 150 square miles and swept through parts of a ski resort. The post Cottonwood Fire Chars Utah appeared first on NASA Science .
